--- date: 2026-04-10T00:00:00+10:00 researcher: claude git_commit: e37178a3b7148b92e12657fe773021945a71a0fc branch: main topic: "Bind Page Rework — Moving GitHub Binding into Org Onboarding Flow" tags: [research, codebase, bind, onboarding, org-creation, github-binding, team-switcher, nuqs, route-groups] status: complete last_updated: 2026-04-10 last_updated_note: "Added design decisions + solution architecture for conditional layout rendering and TeamSwitcher binding badge" --- # Research: Bind Page Rework — Moving GitHub Binding into Org Onboarding Flow **Date**: 2026-04-10 **Git Commit**: e37178a3b7148b92e12657fe773021945a71a0fc **Branch**: main ## Research Question The current bind page (`/account/teams/new/bind?slug=...`) sits under the `(user)/(pending-allowed)` route group. The UX is confusing — it shows "Connect your GitHub organization" but doesn't tell the user which org they're in. Since `setActive()` has already been called by the team creation step, the user is no longer pending. Should binding move into `(org)/[slug]/` under a new onboarding route group? How would this work with the existing binding gate in the org layout? What's needed in `TeamSwitcher` to surface incomplete setup? ## Summary The current org creation flow is a 2-step sequence: **create team** (`/account/teams/new`) → **bind GitHub** (`/account/teams/new/bind?slug=...`). After step 1, the Clerk session is already active with the new org (via `setActive()`), so the user is no longer "pending" — the `(pending-allowed)` route group is only needed for the create step, not the bind step. The bind page uses raw `useSearchParams()` to read the `slug` query param rather than the established `nuqs` library (already used extensively in the codebase). The org layout at `(org)/[slug]/layout.tsx:54-58` has a binding gate that redirects to the bind page if no active binding exists — this creates a **redirect loop hazard** if bind were naively moved inside `(org)/[slug]/`. The `TeamSwitcher` currently has no concept of binding status; it renders all orgs identically with no badge or indicator for incomplete setup. ## Detailed Findings ### 1. Current Bind Page (`(user)/(pending-allowed)/account/teams/new/bind/page.tsx`) A client component wrapped in ``. The `BindContent` component: - Reads `slug` from `useSearchParams()` (line 19) — raw Next.js API, not `nuqs` - Renders "Connect your GitHub organization" with no reference to which org the user just created - Uses `useOAuthPopup({ provider: "github", ... })` to open a popup for GitHub App installation - On success, navigates to `/${slug}` (line 44-45) - Listens on `BroadcastChannel("oauth-connections")` for binding errors (lines 24-35) - Error messages map: `github_org_bound_to_other` and `org_already_bound` (lines 9-14) - If `slug` is falsy, renders nothing (line 55-57) ### 2. Current Team Creation Flow (`teams/new/_components/team-name-form.tsx`) - `TeamNameForm` calls `trpc.organization.create` with `{ slug }` (line 31) - On success: calls `setActive({ organization: data.organizationId })` (lines 33-35) - Then invalidates `listUserOrganizations` query (lines 36-39) - Then navigates to `/account/teams/new/bind?slug=${data.slug}` (line 40) **Key observation**: `setActive()` at line 34 transitions the Clerk session from `pending` to `active` with the new org. After this call completes, the user has a valid org session and is no longer gated by `isPendingAllowedRoute` in the middleware. ### 3. Org Layout Binding Gate (`(org)/[slug]/layout.tsx:42-58`) ``` Check 1 — requireOrgAccess(slug) → notFound() on failure Check 2 — getGitHubBinding(org.id) → redirect to bind page if no active binding ``` The binding check at lines 54-58: ```typescript const binding = await getGitHubBinding(org.id); if (!binding || binding.status !== "active") { redirect(`/account/teams/new/bind?slug=${slug}` as Route); } ``` **Redirect loop hazard**: If the bind page were moved to `(org)/[slug]/(onboarding)/bind/page.tsx`, it would inherit this layout. The layout would see no active binding → redirect to bind page → infinite loop. Any move of bind into the org route group requires restructuring the binding check. ### 4. `getGitHubBinding` Implementation (`apps/app/src/lib/github-binding.ts`) React `cache()`-wrapped function that queries `githubOrgBindings` table by `clerkOrgId`: ```typescript export const getGitHubBinding = cache(async (clerkOrgId: string) => { const rows = await db .select({ id, status, githubOrgLogin, githubOrgAvatarUrl }) .from(githubOrgBindings) .where(eq(githubOrgBindings.clerkOrgId, clerkOrgId)) .limit(1); return rows[0] ?? null; }); ``` Returns binding with `status`, `githubOrgLogin`, and `githubOrgAvatarUrl` — these display fields are available for showing which GitHub org is bound (or being bound to). ### 5. Route Group Architecture ``` (app)/layout.tsx ← NuqsAdapter, TRPCReactProvider, prefetch orgs+account ├── (user)/layout.tsx ← UserPageHeader (TeamSwitcher in account mode) │ └── (pending-allowed)/ ← no layout.tsx, route-group-only concept │ ├── account/welcome/ ← post-login relay │ ├── account/teams/new/ ← create team form │ └── account/teams/new/bind/ ← GitHub bind page (current location) │ └── (org)/[slug]/layout.tsx ← requireOrgAccess + getGitHubBinding gate, sidebar shell ├── page.tsx ← org home (mailbox + ask) └── (manage)/layout.tsx ← centered max-w-5xl wrapper ├── entities/ ├── events/ ├── jobs/ ├── sources/ └── settings/ ``` **Middleware rules** (`proxy.ts:49-55, 82-84`): - `isPendingAllowedRoute` matches `/account/(.*)` and `/api/trpc/organization.create(.*)` - Pending sessions redirected to `/account/welcome` unless route is pending-allowed - `organizationSyncOptions.organizationPatterns: ["/:slug", "/:slug/(.*)"]` syncs active org from URL ### 6. `nuqs` in the Codebase `nuqs` v2.8.9 is already a dependency of `apps/app`. The `NuqsAdapter` wraps the entire `(app)` subtree at `(app)/layout.tsx:13-24`. **Established patterns**: | Pattern | Example | Files | |---------|---------|-------| | Server-side `createLoader` + `createSerializer` | Auth search params with `parseAsString`, `parseAsStringLiteral` | `(auth)/_lib/search-params.ts` | | Client-side `useQueryState` | Individual params with `parseAsString.withDefault(...).withOptions({ shallow: true })` | `components/use-job-filters.ts` | | Client-side `useQueryStates` | Batched params with `parseAsStringEnum` | `(manage)/events/_components/use-event-filters.ts` | The bind page currently uses raw `useSearchParams()` — inconsistent with the rest of the codebase. ### 7. TeamSwitcher Component (`packages/ui/src/components/app-header/team-switcher.tsx`) **Props interface** (lines 25-38): - `createTeamHref: string` - `mode?: "organization" | "account"` - `onOrgSelect: (orgId: string, orgSlug: string) => Promise` - `organizations: Organization[]` where `Organization = { id, name, slug }` **Current org detection** (lines 53-63): Extracts slug from `usePathname()`, checks against hardcoded `reservedRoutes: ["new", "account", "api", "sign-in", "sign-up"]`. **Rendering** (lines 83-182): - Avatar with initials + org name (clickable Link when in org mode) - Dropdown with all orgs, check icon on selected - "Create Team" link at the bottom **No binding status awareness**: The `Organization` type has only `{ id, name, slug }`. There's no field for binding status. No badge, dot, or indicator exists for incomplete setup. **Consumed by**: - `AppSidebar` (line 164-169) — in `(org)/[slug]/layout.tsx` sidebar, mode derived from pathname - `UserPageHeader` (line 59-64) — in `(user)/layout.tsx`, always `mode="account"` ### 8. Existing Status Indicator Patterns The only status indicator pattern in the codebase is in `installed-sources.tsx`: - Green/amber `Circle` dot (lines 237-244) for active vs `awaiting_config` resources - `AlertTriangle` + amber banner for "Configuration Required" state (lines 263-286) - `Badge` component from `@repo/ui` with variants: `default`, `secondary`, `destructive`, `outline` ### 9. `organization.create` tRPC Mutation (`api/app/src/router/user/organization.ts:56-126`) `userScopedProcedure` — accepts both `clerk-pending` and `clerk-active` auth types. Creates a Clerk org with `name: slug, slug: slug, createdBy: userId`. Returns `{ organizationId, slug }`. Handles duplicate slug errors with `TRPCError({ code: "CONFLICT" })`. ### 10. GitHub Binding Enforcement (`api/platform/src/lib/github-binding.ts:77-119`) `enforceGitHubBinding(orgId, githubOrgId)` runs two sequential DB reads: 1. Query by `githubOrgId` → if same org: `reinstate`; if different org: `reject` with `github_org_bound_to_other` 2. Query by `clerkOrgId` → if found: `reject` with `org_already_bound` 3. Neither found → `create` The binding is written during OAuth callback at `api/platform/src/lib/oauth/callback.ts:363-421`. ## Code References - `apps/app/src/app/(app)/(user)/(pending-allowed)/account/teams/new/bind/page.tsx` — current bind page - `apps/app/src/app/(app)/(user)/(pending-allowed)/account/teams/new/_components/team-name-form.tsx:33-40` — `setActive()` + redirect to bind - `apps/app/src/app/(app)/(org)/[slug]/layout.tsx:54-58` — binding gate with redirect - `apps/app/src/lib/github-binding.ts:5-17` — `getGitHubBinding()` server function - `apps/app/src/hooks/use-oauth-popup.ts` — OAuth popup mechanics - `packages/ui/src/components/app-header/team-switcher.tsx` — TeamSwitcher component - `apps/app/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx:164-169` — TeamSwitcher consumed in sidebar - `apps/app/src/components/user-page-header.tsx:59-64` — TeamSwitcher consumed in user header - `apps/app/src/proxy.ts:49-55` — `isPendingAllowedRoute` middleware matcher - `apps/app/src/app/(app)/layout.tsx:13` — `NuqsAdapter` wrapping the app tree - `api/app/src/router/user/organization.ts:56-126` — `organization.create` mutation - `api/platform/src/lib/oauth/callback.ts:363-421` — binding write during OAuth callback - `api/platform/src/lib/github-binding.ts:77-119` — `enforceGitHubBinding` constraint logic - `db/app/src/schema/tables/github-org-bindings.ts` — `githubOrgBindings` table schema ## Architecture Documentation ### Current Flow (2 steps, 2 route groups) ``` /account/teams/new (user)(pending-allowed) ↓ setActive() + redirect /account/teams/new/bind?slug=X (user)(pending-allowed) ↓ OAuth popup → onSuccess /X (org)/[slug] ``` ### Binding Gate in Org Layout The `[slug]/layout.tsx` binding check means every org route load verifies binding status server-side. Unbound orgs always redirect to the bind page. This is the enforcement point — the bind page itself is the recovery path. ### Session State Across Steps | Step | Clerk Session | Route Group | Middleware Status | |------|--------------|-------------|------------------| | Welcome relay | pending or active | `(pending-allowed)` | pending allowed | | Create team | pending → active (after `setActive`) | `(pending-allowed)` | pending allowed | | Bind GitHub | **active** (org is set) | `(pending-allowed)` | would pass org routes too | | Org workspace | active | `(org)/[slug]` | active required | The bind step runs under `(pending-allowed)` but the session is already active. The user could technically navigate to `/{slug}` at this point — the org layout's binding check would catch them and redirect back to bind. ### Key Constraint for Moving Bind The redirect at `[slug]/layout.tsx:57` sends unbound orgs to `/account/teams/new/bind?slug=${slug}`. If bind moves inside the `[slug]` tree: 1. The layout's binding check must be restructured — either a new route group that skips the check, or the check must be made conditional on the child route 2. Next.js route groups like `(onboarding)` could have their own layout that bypasses the binding check, but `[slug]/layout.tsx` runs for ALL child routes regardless of route groups 3. The binding check would need to move from the `[slug]/layout.tsx` into the `(manage)` or workspace layouts instead, with `(onboarding)` routes left unguarded ## Historical Context - `thoughts/shared/research/2026-04-09-org-creation-github-binding-architecture.md` — documents the earlier architecture before `githubOrgBindings` table existed. The binding flow has since been implemented with the `enforceGitHubBinding` constraint system and the current bind page. ## Related Research - `thoughts/shared/research/2026-04-09-org-creation-github-binding-architecture.md` — prior org creation architecture ## Design Decisions (2026-04-10) 1. **Binding check stays at auth level** — must gate everything under `[slug]`. If GitHub org is removed/revoked, everything halts immediately. 2. **Bind only for now** — no multi-step onboarding. Just GitHub bind. 3. **Binding is mandatory** — cannot be skipped. No org feature works without active binding. 4. **Efficient querying** — TeamSwitcher binding data should use best practices (batch query, shared cache). ## Solution Architecture ### Conditional Layout Rendering (No Redirect, No Loop) The `[slug]/layout.tsx` checks binding and renders a **different shell** instead of redirecting: ```typescript // (org)/[slug]/layout.tsx export default async function OrgLayout({ children, params }) { const { slug } = await params; let org: OrgWithAccess["org"]; try { const access = await requireOrgAccess(slug); org = access.org; } catch { notFound(); } const binding = await getGitHubBinding(org.id); // Unbound → render setup shell (no sidebar, no workspace) if (!binding || binding.status !== "active") { return ( ); } // Bound → full workspace return (
{children}
); } ``` **Why this works:** - Binding check is at `[slug]` level — gates everything - No redirect loop — layout renders different UI based on state - Bind UI inherits full org context (`org.name`, `slug`) from `requireOrgAccess` — no `?slug=` query param needed - URL is `/{slug}` — clean - If binding revoked, everything halts on next load - Future steps (invite) can extend the setup shell without new routes ### New Flow ``` /account/teams/new (user)(pending-allowed) — create team, setActive() ↓ redirect to /{slug} /{slug} (org)/[slug] — layout detects no binding → renders GitHubBindStep ↓ OAuth popup → onSuccess → page reload /{slug} (org)/[slug] — layout detects active binding → renders workspace ``` **Key change**: `team-name-form.tsx` onSuccess redirects to `/${data.slug}` instead of `/account/teams/new/bind?slug=...`. The org layout handles the rest. ### Delete - `apps/app/src/app/(app)/(user)/(pending-allowed)/account/teams/new/bind/` — entire directory, no longer needed ### TeamSwitcher Binding Badge — Efficient Query #### Extend `listUserOrganizations` with batch DB join `listUserOrganizations` (`api/app/src/router/user/organization.ts:24-47`) is currently pure Clerk API — returns `{ id, slug, name, role, imageUrl }[]`. Add one DB query: ```typescript // After fetching Clerk memberships, batch-query binding status const orgIds = memberships.map(m => m.organization.id); const bindings = await db .select({ clerkOrgId: githubOrgBindings.clerkOrgId, status: githubOrgBindings.status, }) .from(githubOrgBindings) .where(inArray(githubOrgBindings.clerkOrgId, orgIds)); const bindingMap = new Map(bindings.map(b => [b.clerkOrgId, b.status])); // Merge into response return memberships.map(m => ({ id: m.organization.id, slug: m.organization.slug, name: m.organization.name, role: m.role, imageUrl: m.organization.imageUrl, githubBound: bindingMap.get(m.organization.id) === "active", })); ``` **Efficiency:** - Single `SELECT ... WHERE clerk_org_id IN (...)` — indexed on unique `clerk_org_id` column - Co-located with existing Clerk call — one additional SQL query per cache miss - Shared via React Query cache with existing 5-min `staleTime` — all consumers (`app-sidebar.tsx`, `user-page-header.tsx`, `use-active-org.ts`) get binding status for free - No additional network requests from any consumer #### TeamSwitcher UI change Extend `Organization` type in `packages/ui/src/components/app-header/team-switcher.tsx`: ```typescript interface Organization { id: string; name: string; slug: string | null; githubBound?: boolean; // new — undefined for backwards compat } ``` In the dropdown, show an indicator for unbound orgs: - Amber dot (matching existing `installed-sources.tsx` pattern at lines 237-244) next to org name - Or a small `Badge variant="destructive"` with "Setup" text ### Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `apps/app/src/app/(app)/(org)/[slug]/layout.tsx` | Replace redirect with conditional rendering | | `apps/app/src/app/(app)/(user)/(pending-allowed)/account/teams/new/_components/team-name-form.tsx:40` | Change redirect target from `/account/teams/new/bind?slug=...` to `/${slug}` | | `api/app/src/router/user/organization.ts:24-47` | Add batch `githubOrgBindings` query, return `githubBound` field | | `packages/ui/src/components/app-header/team-switcher.tsx` | Extend `Organization` type, add badge/dot for unbound orgs | | `apps/app/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx` | Pass `githubBound` through to TeamSwitcher (already gets data from `listUserOrganizations`) | | `apps/app/src/components/user-page-header.tsx` | Same — pass `githubBound` through | ### Files to Delete | File | Reason | |------|--------| | `apps/app/src/app/(app)/(user)/(pending-allowed)/account/teams/new/bind/page.tsx` | Replaced by `GitHubBindStep` component rendered in layout | ### New Files | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `apps/app/src/components/github-bind-step.tsx` | Client component extracted from old bind page — OAuth popup + org context display | ## Open Questions (All original questions resolved by design decisions above — none remaining.)
